Research the following college application platforms. For each one, I need:
1. The official website URL
2. Whether they have a public member institution list/search page
3. The URL of that list page
4. Approximate number of member institutions
5. Whether the list appears scrapable (is it server-rendered HTML, or does it load via JavaScript/API calls?)

Platforms to research:

**National platforms:**
- Common App (commonapp.org)
- Coalition App / Scoir (coalitionforcollegeaccess.org or scoir.com)
- Black Common Application (blackcommonapp.org or similar)
- Universal College Application
- QuestBridge National College Match (questbridge.org)

**State-specific systems:**
- ApplyTexas (applytexas.org)
- Apply SUNY (suny.edu)
- UC Application (universityofcalifornia.edu)
- CSU Application (calstate.edu)
- Georgia state schools application system
- Florida state schools application system
- North Carolina state schools application system
- Wisconsin state schools application system
- Idaho state schools application system

Use WebSearch and WebFetch to find and verify each platform's member list page. For the state-specific ones, find out what the actual application portal is called and whether it covers all public institutions in that state.

Return a detailed report with findings for each platform.

Based on the search results, I found information about the Common Black College Application and its member institutions:

## Overview

The Common Black College App (CBCA) is much like the Common App – students can use it to apply to several schools at the same time, saving countless hours and potentially hundreds of dollars. There are more than 70 participating Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), and for a one-time fee of $20, students can apply to all participating institutions (over 70) with a single application.

## Finding the Complete List

The search results reference a member institutions list, but the actual detailed list wasn't fully displayed in the search results. However, I can point you to the official source: The official Common Black College App website (commonblackcollegeapp.com) has a "LIST OF MEMBER INSTITUTIONS" page where you can view the complete, current list of all participating schools.

## Notable Information

It's worth noting that if you're interested in applying to Spelman, Howard, or Morehouse—three of the most well-known HBCUs in the country—you'll have to use the Common Application, as they don't accept the CBCA.

For the most up-to-date and comprehensive list of member colleges, I recommend visiting the official Common Black College Application website directly at commonblackcollegeapp.com/schools.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Universal College Application member schools:

## Current Status

The Universal College Application is accepted by 1 higher education institution in the United States (as of November 2024), representing a significant decline from its earlier years. 77 institutions used the service in 2010, but the UCA currently serves 18 institutions according to more recent data.

## Notable Member Schools

Many big name schools in the US use the Universal College Application, including three of the Ivy League schools – Cornell, Harvard, and Princeton. Other schools mentioned as members include Bard College at Simon's Rock and various regional institutions.

## Key Information

- The Universal College Application (UCA) is a consortium application that allows students to use one application to apply to many colleges and universities
- 21 states, plus Bulgaria, have colleges that accept the Universal College Application, with New York and Massachusetts having the most UCA schools

For the most current and comprehensive list of all member institutions, you can visit the official Universal College Application website at universalcollegeapp.com, which should have the complete up-to-date listing of member schools.

I've completed a web search for "UC Application University of California campuses apply". Here are the key findings:

## UC Application Overview

All University of California campuses use the UC Application. All UC campuses are now open for the fall 2026 application.

## UC Campuses

The University of California system consists of the following schools: UCLA, UC San Diego, UC Santa Barbara, UC Santa Cruz, UC Merced, UC Berkeley, UC Davis, UC Riverside, and UC Irvine. You can apply to one campus or multiple campuses (up to all 9).

## Application Fees

The application fee is $80 for each UC campus ($95 for international and non-immigrant applicants). However, UC will waive application fees for up to four campuses for qualified students who would otherwise be unable to apply for admission.

## Key Application Details

- Unlike the Common App, the UC application process requires students to self-report their academic history and test scores as opposed to submitting a copy of their academic transcript.
- UC will not consider SAT or ACT test scores when making admissions decisions or awarding scholarships.
- The University of California (UC) application opens August 1 and the period of time to submit an application for admission is October 1–November 30.

For more detailed information, you can visit the official UC Admissions website at https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/ or the application portal at https://apply.universityofcalifornia.edu/.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the CSU Cal State Apply application portal and campuses:

## Cal State Apply Portal

Cal State Apply is a Centralized Application Service (CAS) that simplifies the process of applying to California State University (CSU) programs. You start by selecting the programs you wish to apply to, then you submit one application that includes all necessary materials. With one application, you can apply to multiple CSU campuses and keep your options open.

## Number of Campuses

The CSU has 22 campuses, though a transition to 22 universities is in progress (Cal Poly and Cal Maritime integrating)—official Fall 2026.

## Application Features

The application process is simple: no essays, no test scores for most applicants. The fee to apply to the CSU is $70 per campus.

The official Cal State Apply portal can be accessed at **www.calstate.edu/apply**, where you can find more information about all available campuses and programs.

I now have enough information to compile the full report. Here it is:

---

# College Application Platform Research Report

## NATIONAL PLATFORMS

### 1. Common App

- **Website**: https://www.commonapp.org/
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes
- **List URL**: https://www.commonapp.org/explore/
- **Approximate member institutions**: 1,100+
  - 307+ public universities, all 50 states + DC + Puerto Rico + international schools, 12 HBCUs, 400+ with no application fee
- **Scrapability**: The `/explore/` page returned only CSS/Bootstrap when fetched, indicating it is a **JavaScript-rendered SPA** (likely React). The college data loads dynamically via client-side API calls after page load. A headless browser (Playwright/Puppeteer) would be required to scrape it. Common App also has a partner API at https://partners.commonapp.org/ which may provide structured data. There is also a PDF grid of requirements at https://content.commonapp.org/Files/ReqGrid.pdf and a Salesforce-based support page at https://appsupport.commonapp.org/applicantsupport/s/live-common-app-members-a-c (also JS-rendered).

---

### 2. Coalition App / Scoir

- **Website**: https://www.coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/ (Coalition) / https://www.scoir.com/ (Scoir, the application platform)
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes, two separate lists
- **List URLs**:
  - Coalition members: https://www.coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/our-members (all Coalition members)
  - Coalition by category: https://www.coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/member-schools-category/ (public vs private)
  - Scoir app-receiving colleges: https://www.scoir.com/apply-colleges (colleges that accept applications via Scoir)
- **Approximate member institutions**: ~150 Coalition members; 123 colleges currently accepting applications via Scoir (as of March 2026, growing weekly)
- **Scrapability**:
  - **Coalition members page**: Hosted on Squarespace. The alphabetical list appears partially server-rendered in HTML but also uses a jQuery AJAX call to `https://access.coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/manage/query/run?id=c4617c7a-...` to load school data from an external API. That API endpoint could potentially be called directly for structured JSON data.
  - **Scoir apply-colleges page**: **Server-rendered HTML** with all 123 college names in the page source. Highly scrapable.

---

### 3. Common Black College Application (CBCA)

- **Website**: https://commonblackcollegeapp.com/
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes
- **List URL**: https://commonblackcollegeapp.com/schools/
- **Approximate member institutions**: ~50-70 HBCUs (sources vary; the site says "over 50", some references say "over 70")
- **Scrapability**: The fetched page did not return the actual school list in the HTML -- it showed navigation and login elements. The school data may require JavaScript execution or may be behind the application interface. **Likely requires a headless browser** to extract. Notable: Spelman, Howard, and Morehouse do NOT participate (they use Common App instead).

---

### 4. Universal College Application (UCA)

- **Website**: https://www.universalcollegeapp.com/
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes
- **List URL**: https://www.universalcollegeapp.com/schools
- **Approximate member institutions**: **1** (University of Charleston, WV). Declined from 77 in 2010.
- **Scrapability**: **Server-rendered HTML** (hybrid with some client-side search JS). Easily scrapable, though with only 1 school there is little to scrape. This platform is effectively defunct.

---

### 5. QuestBridge National College Match

- **Website**: https://www.questbridge.org/
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes
- **List URL**: https://www.questbridge.org/partners/college-partners
- **Approximate member institutions**: **55** partner colleges
- **Scrapability**: **Server-rendered HTML** with all 55 colleges, their locations, and enrollment figures directly embedded in the page markup. Highly scrapable. The complete list includes institutions from Amherst College through Yale University (all highly selective schools).

---

## STATE-SPECIFIC SYSTEMS

### 6. ApplyTexas

- **Website**: https://www.applytexas.org/
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes
- **List URL**: https://www.applytexas.org/school-search/
- **Approximate member institutions**: ~90 (all Texas public universities + many community colleges + some private universities). Covers all UT System, Texas A&M System, Texas State University System, and many private schools.
- **Scrapability**: The school search page returned a 403 on direct fetch. The portal is managed by the Texas Higher Education Coordinating Board. There is also an older search URL at `https://www.applytexas.org/atx/school_search?s_sch_search_type=C&s_app_occ=1`. **Likely requires a headless browser or may block automated access**. Covers all public institutions in the state.

---

### 7. Apply SUNY (New York)

- **Portal name**: ApplySUNY
- **Website**: https://www.suny.edu/applysuny/
- **Public member list/search page**: Yes
- **List URL**: https://www.suny.edu/attend/apply-to-suny/participating-campuses/
- **Non-participating list**: https://www.suny.edu/attend/apply-to-suny/non-participating-campuses/
- **Approximate member institutions**: ~60 of the 64 SUNY campuses participate (a few do not accept ApplySUNY and have their own applications)
- **Scrapability**: The participating campuses page returned a 403 on direct fetch. The SUNY site may require a browser. However, SUNY has a well-defined system of 64 campuses that is well-documented elsewhere. **Covers nearly all public institutions** in New York state.

---

### 8. UC Application (California - University of California)

- **Portal name**: UC Application
- **Website**: https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/
- **Application portal**: https://apply.universityofcalifornia.edu/
- **Public member list**: The list is fixed and well-known -- no dynamic search page needed
- **Approximate member institutions**: **9 undergraduate campuses**: UCLA, UC Berkeley, UC San Diego, UC Davis, UC Santa Barbara, UC Irvine, UC Riverside, UC Santa Cruz, UC Merced
- **Scrapability**: N/A -- the list is static and well-documented. The campus list is on https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/. **Server-rendered, easily obtainable.** Covers all UC campuses (which are all the top-tier public research universities in CA).

---

### 9. Cal State Apply (California - CSU)

- **Portal name**: Cal State Apply
- **Website**: https://www.calstate.edu/apply
- **Application portal**: https://calstate.cas.myliaison.com/ (hosted by Liaison/CAS)
- **Public campus list**: https://www.calstate.edu/attend/campuses and https://www.calstate.edu/attend/campuses/find-your-csu
- **Approximate member institutions**: **23 campuses** (transitioning to 22 as Cal Poly and Cal Maritime merge)
- **Scrapability**: The calstate.edu campus pages returned 403 on direct fetch. However, the list is static and well-documented. The Liaison-hosted application portal is a separate system. **Covers all CSU campuses** in California.

---

### 10. Georgia -- GAfutures / GEORGIA MATCH

- **Portal name**: GAfutures + GEORGIA MATCH (direct admissions program)
- **Website**: https://www.gafutures.org/
- **GEORGIA MATCH**: https://www.gafutures.org/college-planning/georgia-match
- **Public institution list**: https://www.gafutures.org/college-planning/college-search/state-of-georgia-colleges/university-system-of-georgia/
- **Approximate member institutions**: 26 University System of Georgia (USG) institutions + 22 Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG) institutions = ~48 total in GEORGIA MATCH. Note: Georgia Tech, UGA, and Georgia College are NOT in GEORGIA MATCH (holistic admissions). There is **no single unified application portal** -- each USG institution has its own application. GEORGIA MATCH is a direct admissions initiative, not an application platform.
- **Scrapability**: The GAfutures USG list page is **server-rendered HTML** with all 26 USG institutions as cards. Highly scrapable. Does NOT cover private institutions.

---

### 11. Florida -- State University System (SUS)

- **Portal name**: **No unified application portal exists**
- **Board of Governors website**: https://www.flbog.edu/
- **Approximate member institutions**: 12 state universities in the SUS (UF, FSU, UCF, USF, FIU, FAU, FGCU, UNF, UWF, FAMU, FPOLY/FPU, NCF)
- **How students apply**: Each of the 12 SUS universities maintains its own application portal. Most also accept Common App and/or Coalition App. There is no Florida-specific common application.
- **Scrapability**: N/A -- no centralized list page to scrape. The 12 universities are a well-known fixed list.

---

### 12. North Carolina -- CFNC / NC College Connect

- **Portal name**: CFNC (College for North Carolina) + NC College Connect
- **Website**: https://www.cfnc.org/
- **College search**: https://www2.cfnc.org/college-search
- **NC College Connect**: https://www.cfnc.org/nc-college-connect/ (simplified application for UNC System schools)
- **UNC System institutions list**: https://www.northcarolina.edu/institutions/
- **Approximate member institutions**: CFNC covers all ~110 NC public and private colleges. The UNC System has **17 institutions** (16 universities + NC School of Science and Mathematics). NC also has 58 community colleges.
- **Scrapability**:
  - **CFNC college search**: Uses Angular.js -- **client-side rendered**, would require headless browser.
  - **UNC System institutions page**: **Server-rendered HTML** with all 17 institutions as structured cards. Highly scrapable.
  - CFNC is not a unified application portal per se -- NC College Connect provides a simplified process for select UNC System schools, but students still apply through individual institutional portals or Common App.

---

### 13. Wisconsin -- Universities of Wisconsin

- **Portal name**: Universities of Wisconsin Admission Pathway Portal
- **Website**: https://apply.wisconsin.edu/ (application portal)
- **Campus list**: https://www.wisconsin.edu/students/apply-now/
- **Approximate member institutions**: **13 UW campuses** (UW-Eau Claire, UW-Green Bay, UW-La Crosse, UW-Madison, UW-Milwaukee, UW-Oshkosh, UW-Parkside, UW-Platteville, UW-River Falls, UW-Stevens Point, UW-Stout, UW-Superior, UW-Whitewater)
- **Scrapability**:
  - **apply.wisconsin.edu**: JavaScript SPA ("You need to enable JavaScript to run this app"). Not scrapable without headless browser.
  - **wisconsin.edu/students/apply-now/**: **Server-rendered HTML** with all 13 campuses, schema.org JSON-LD data, and consistent link patterns. Highly scrapable.
  - This is a true unified application portal -- one application for multiple UW campuses. Covers **all public universities** in Wisconsin.

---

### 14. Idaho -- Apply Idaho / Next Steps Idaho

- **Portal name**: Apply Idaho (part of Next Steps Idaho)
- **Website**: https://nextsteps.idaho.gov/apply-idaho
- **Application portal**: https://apply.nextsteps.idaho.gov/
- **College directory**: https://nextsteps.idaho.gov/college-and-universities-directory
- **Campus choice**: https://nextsteps.idaho.gov/campus-choice
- **Approximate member institutions**: ~10 public colleges/universities (Boise State University, Idaho State University, University of Idaho, Lewis-Clark State College, College of Southern Idaho, College of Western Idaho, Eastern Idaho Technical College, North Idaho College, and others). Apply Idaho is **free and limited to current-year graduating Idaho high school seniors**.
- **Scrapability**: The site is a **React/Material-UI SPA** -- client-side rendered. The college directory page did not return institution data in the HTML. **Requires a headless browser** or API discovery. Covers all public institutions in Idaho.

---

## Summary Table

| Platform | URL | Public List? | # Institutions | Scrapable? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Common App | commonapp.org/explore | Yes | ~1,100 | JS SPA -- headless browser needed |
| Coalition/Scoir | coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/our-members | Yes | ~150 / 123 (Scoir) | Scoir list is server HTML; Coalition uses API |
| Common Black College App | commonblackcollegeapp.com/schools | Yes | ~50-70 | Likely JS -- headless browser needed |
| Universal College App | universalcollegeapp.com/schools | Yes | 1 | Server HTML (effectively defunct) |
| QuestBridge | questbridge.org/partners/college-partners | Yes | 55 | Server HTML -- easily scrapable |
| ApplyTexas | applytexas.org/school-search | Yes | ~90 | 403 on fetch -- may block bots |
| Apply SUNY | suny.edu/.../participating-campuses | Yes | ~60 of 64 | 403 on fetch -- may block bots |
| UC Application | admission.universityofcalifornia.edu | Fixed list | 9 | Static/trivial |
| Cal State Apply | calstate.edu/attend/campuses | Yes | 23 | 403 on fetch; list is well-known |
| Georgia (GAfutures) | gafutures.org/.../university-system-of-georgia | Yes | 26 USG + 22 TCSG | Server HTML -- easily scrapable |
| Florida (SUS) | flbog.edu | No unified portal | 12 | No centralized list to scrape |
| North Carolina (CFNC) | cfnc.org, northcarolina.edu/institutions | Yes | 17 UNC + 58 CC | UNC list is server HTML; CFNC is Angular SPA |
| Wisconsin (UW) | wisconsin.edu/students/apply-now | Yes | 13 | Server HTML -- easily scrapable |
| Idaho (Apply Idaho) | nextsteps.idaho.gov/apply-idaho | Yes | ~10 | React SPA -- headless browser needed |

### Key takeaways for scrapability:

**Easily scrapable (server-rendered HTML):**
- QuestBridge partner colleges
- Scoir app-receiving colleges list
- GAfutures USG institution list
- UNC System institutions page
- Wisconsin UW campus list

**Requires headless browser (JS SPA):**
- Common App explore page
- Common Black College App schools page
- Apply Idaho / Next Steps Idaho
- CFNC college search

**May block automated access (403 errors):**
- ApplyTexas school search
- Apply SUNY participating campuses
- Cal State campus pages

**Static/trivial (fixed, well-known lists):**
- UC Application (9 campuses)
- Florida SUS (12 universities, no unified portal)
